CREATE TABLE data_points (id INTEGER, x FLOAT, y FLOAT);
SELECT regr_syy(y, x) FROM data_points WHERE x IS NOT NULL AND y IS NOT NULL;
SELECT regr_syy(y, x) FROM (SELECT x, y FROM data_points WHERE y IS NOT NULL) AS filtered;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (id INTEGER, x FLOAT, y FLOAT);
SELECT regr_syy(y, x) FROM data_points JOIN additional_data ON data_points.id = additional_data.id;
SELECT AVG(regr_syy(y, x)) FROM (SELECT y, x FROM data_points WHERE x IS NOT NULL) AS avg_syy;
SELECT regr_syy(y, x) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT x, y FROM data_points) AS unique_pairs;
SELECT a.x, regr_syy(a.y, a.x) AS variance FROM data_points a WHERE a.x IS NOT NULL GROUP BY a.x;
SELECT a.x, regr_syy(a.y, a.x) AS variance FROM data_points a JOIN additional_data b ON a.id = b.id WHERE a.y IS NOT NULL AND b.y IS NOT NULL GROUP BY a.x;
